citywideContext: NYC median 1BR crossed $4,000 for the first time in 2026; Manhattan median 1BR is roughly $4,400–4,700 and climbing, and inventory is at a ~4-year low — move fast on good listings.

east-village.note: Walkups dominate at $4K; large-building averages run ~$5.6K (Zumper).

lower-east-side.note: Tenement walkups list in the high-$3Ks; new towers skew the average.
lower-east-side.whatFourKGets: A tenement walkup in the high-$3Ks — the $5.4K average is skewed by new towers.

williamsburg.note: Big buildings average ~$5,286; the +12% YoY figure applies to large buildings (RentCafe, Apr 2026).
williamsburg.whatFourKGets: Under the $4,850 median — older or smaller units, not the big new buildings.

east-williamsburg.whatFourKGets: Just under the $4,350 median, but rents are rising fast at +18% a year.

greenpoint.whatFourKGets: Under the $4,675 median — smaller 1BRs, though rents have held flat.

bushwick.whatFourKGets: $4K rents above the median here — pick of the litter.

astoria.whatFourKGets: $4K is well above the $3,400 median — top-of-market space and choice.

long-island-city.note: Luxury-heavy inventory; verify via RentCast at build time.
long-island-city.whatFourKGets: The bottom of a luxury-heavy market — most inventory is new towers at $4.1K and up.

hells-kitchen.whatFourKGets: Under the $4,900 average — you trade square footage for the walk to work.

yorkville.note: UES median $4,725 all unit types; value pocket of Manhattan.
yorkville.whatFourKGets: A real shot at a 1BR — the range starts right at $4,000 in this Manhattan value pocket.
